info.title: EnergyGrid Usage API
info.description:
The **EnergyGrid Usage API** is the customer-facing read API behind the EnergyGrid app and partner web experiences. It exposes the energy consumption, budgeting, and insight surfaces that customers see — authenticated as the customer, not the utility.

Where the [PowerBox API](/docs/api/powerbox-api) handles utility-to-platform integration (CIS sync, hardware binding), the Usage API handles customer-to-platform reads (usage history, budget management, AI-powered insights).

## Audience

This API is intended for partner mobile apps and white-labelled web portals that surface energy usage to end customers. All requests are made on behalf of an authenticated customer.

## Authentication

Clients exchange a utility identity token for an EnergyGrid customer token via `POST /Customer/login`. The returned token is bearer-format and scoped to the requesting customer — it cannot read data for other accounts. See the [Integration Guide — Configure authentication (OAuth)](/docs/integration-guide#step-2-configure-authentication-oauth) for the full handshake.

## Base URL

```
https://api.energygrid.example/usage/v1
```

## Conventions

- **Content type.** Requests and responses use `application/json`.
- **Time series granularity.** Usage endpoints accept a `granularity` parameter — values come from `GET /Metadata/granularity-types`. Don't hard-code.
- **Currency and units.** Monetary fields are returned in the customer's billing currency, energy fields in the meter's `unitOfMeasure` (typically `kWh` for electric, `therms` for gas).
- **Pagination.** List endpoints page via `pageSize` and `pageToken`. The next token is returned in the response body when more results are available.
- **Errors.** Errors follow [RFC 7807](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7807) Problem Details, content type `application/problem+json`.

## Sample

This specification is published as a portfolio sample. The contracts and schemas are representative of the kind of customer-facing usage API that a residential energy management platform would expose. The hostnames are illustrative and do not resolve.

servers.description: Production — illustrative URL, does not resolve
Staging — for integration testing against synthetic data

tags.name: Similar Homes
tags.description: Compare energy usage with similar homes

tags.name: Metadata
tags.description: Reference data for interpreting API responses

post.description: Authenticates a customer using a utility identity system token and returns a EnergyGrid authentication token.

200.description: Success. Returns the EnergyGrid authentication token object.

400.description: Bad request. The token format is invalid.

401.description: Unauthorized. The token couldn't be validated.

404.description: Not found. The customer doesn't exist in the data store.

get.description: Returns customer information including premises and devices (things). Use this endpoint as your starting point after login.

200.description: Success. Returns customer object with premises and things.

401.description: Unauthorized. Invalid or missing authentication token.

get.description: Retrieves customer budget for a specific site.

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: Granularity type: day, week, month, or billingperiod

post.summary: Sets customer budget based on object data
post.description: Sets customer budget based on the provided goal type.

get.summary: Gets customer home profile for given customer and site.
get.description: Retrieves home profile information for a customer site.

post.summary: Creates customer home profile for values in request.
post.description: Updates home profile information for a customer site.

get.description: Gets a detailed breakdown of energy usage by category and subcategory.

get.summary: Retrieves main uses of energy for Customer Site
get.description: Retrieves the primary energy consumers for a customer site.

get.summary: Returns message carousel items for given customer and site.
get.description: Gets personalized messages and tips for the customer.
Compares a customer's energy usage with similar homes in the area.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Start date of the comparison interval

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: End date of the comparison interval

get.description: Retrieves detailed energy usage data with flexible filtering options.

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: Granularity: interval (15-min), hour, day, week, month, or billingperiod

get.description: Gets all supported device and meter types.

200.description: Success. Returns array of thing types.
Success. Returns array of thing categories.

get.description: Gets available time granularities for data queries.

200.description: Success. Returns array of granularity types.

get.description: Gets available property types for things.

200.description: Success. Returns array of property types.
Success. Returns array of metric types.

get.description: Gets rate groupings used for cost calculations.

200.description: Success. Returns array of rate groups.

get.description: Gets billing cycle start and end dates.

200.description: Success. Returns array of billing cycle dates.

token.description: EnergyGrid authentication token for subsequent API calls

clientId.description: Utility's BPN, billing ID, profile, or client ID

externalSiteId.description: Utility's premise, site, or service point location identifier

billingId.description: Billing ID associated with the premise

things.description: Array of devices and meters at this site

minimumGranularityType.description: Minimum granularity type the thing supports

externalDeviceId.description: Utility's meter number or service point number

powerCoreId.description: EnergyGrid's device or service point ID

isUsageLoaded.description: Indicates if usage data is loaded

totalUnit.description: Energy units for this rate group

goalType.description: Budget goal type: standard, eco, or custom
Budget goal type: standard, eco, or custom

customAmount.description: Custom monthly dollar amount (required when goalType is custom)

value.description: Numeric value representing the selection or count

bodyText.example: Consider adjusting your thermostat by 2 degrees to save up to 5% on cooling costs.
